strengths: Demonstrated proper needle loading and driving technique through the Penrose drain
Successfully completed three throws with appropriate extracorporeal knot tying technique
Maintained consistent instrument control throughout the suturing sequence
weaknesses: Failed to complete the final suture cut within the 10-minute time limit
Time management inefficiency led to incomplete task execution
May have spent excessive time on knot tightening or positioning phases

drills.name: Timed completion ladder
drills.description: Complete full task sequences with progressive time targets, starting at 8 minutes and reducing to 6 minutes over multiple sessions.
drills.targets_weakness: Time management and task completion within limits

drills.name: Final phase acceleration drill
drills.description: Practice the knot tightening and suture cutting sequence in isolation, emphasizing efficient completion without compromising security.

drills.name: Economy-of-motion refinement
drills.description: Perform complete task with deliberate focus on eliminating unnecessary movements and optimizing instrument positioning throughout.

progression_note: At the advanced level, consistent completion within time limits is essential for benchmark achievement. Focus on streamlining the final phases while maintaining the technical proficiency already demonstrated in needle driving and knot construction.
